message:
test(btrfs): conformance coverage for prealloc gap handling (bd-z91hc)

Pins the behavior introduced by bd8c4f9: btrfs fallocate over a range
that mixes an existing live extent and a leading hole must allocate a
new unwritten prealloc extent only over the uncovered hole, leave the
live extent's initialized status untouched, and surface as two
non-overlapping ranges through FIEMAP.

Setup
- mkfs btrfs (128 MiB image), open writable.
- Create btrfs_prealloc_gap.bin under root.
- Write a 4 KiB live block at offset 4096 (leaving [0, 4096) as a hole).
- fallocate over [0, 8192) with mode = 0.

Assertions
- fiemap returns exactly two extents.
- extents[0]: logical=0, length=4096, FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN set
  (the new prealloc extent over the leading hole).
- extents[1]: logical=4096, length=4096, FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN unset
  (the pre-existing live data extent stayed initialized).
- extents[0].logical + extents[0].length <= extents[1].logical
  (no overlap).
- read([0, 8192)) returns 4096 zero bytes followed by the original
  live payload — readback is preserved end to end.

Also imports FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN into ffs_types and the FsOps trait
into the conformance test scope, both of which were already in use
elsewhere but not previously brought in for this test module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
